threat_actor.name: DangerDev
threat_actor.motivation: Financial — cryptomining, SES spam/phishing for cryptocurrency credentials, PayPal credential theft via spearphishing

credential_chain.source: Accidentally exposed IAM admin long-term access key in external storage
IAM CreateUser + CreateLoginProfile + AttachUserPolicy for DangerDev@protonmail.me
credential_chain.credential_type: AWS IAM console password + access key (AdministratorAccess)

credential_chain.source: IAM CreateUser + CreateAccessKey for masquerading 'ses' account
IAM CreateRole with external trust policy; AssumeRole from TA-controlled account 671050157472
CreateAccessKey on existing victim IAM accounts
UpdateLoginProfile using initially compromised account to reset another account's console password

service_names: 671050157472 (confirmed malicious AWS account — used for cross-account AssumeRole)
tool_names: ses-smtp-user (legitimate naming convention abused for masquerade)
user_agents: Amazon Relational Database Service console (used anomalously to access GuardDuty)

attack_creates_resources: IAM user: ses (masquerade, with access key)
IAM role: AWSLanding-Zones-ConfigRecorderRoles (cross-account backdoor to account 265857590823)
IAM role: AWSeservedSSO_* (cross-account backdoor to account 671050157472)
EC2 security group (inbound RDP/3389 and SSH/22 open to 0.0.0.0/0)
EC2 instances: t2.micro (test), p3.16xlarge x3 (GPU mining), multiple GPU-enabled instance types
AWS support case (SES sending quota increase request)

cannot_safely_emulate.reason: Sending real phishing emails to real targets is illegal and harms third parties
cannot_safely_emulate.simulation: Log CreateEmailIdentity and SendEmail API calls; verify SES sandbox mode blocks delivery

cannot_safely_emulate.technique: T1583.001 — Route53 RegisterDomain for PayPal-mimicking domains
cannot_safely_emulate.reason: Creates real internet-facing domains with financial and legal implications; domain may be cached/indexed
cannot_safely_emulate.simulation: Document RegisterDomain API call structure; use a clearly non-malicious test domain in a sandboxed account

cannot_safely_emulate.technique: T1199 — Cross-account AssumeRole from TA-controlled accounts 265857590823 / 671050157472
cannot_safely_emulate.reason: Requires access to confirmed malicious external AWS accounts; simulating requires a controlled adversary account
cannot_safely_emulate.simulation: Create isolated adversary AWS account, establish cross-account trust role, and AssumeRole in a lab environment only

cannot_safely_emulate.reason: Extremely expensive ($24+/hr per instance); creates real AWS billing impact and may violate ToS
cannot_safely_emulate.simulation: Launch a t2.micro with a benign CPU-bound workload to validate instance launch/terminate lifecycle only
